sectors.name: Base Materials & Gas Suppliers
sectors.description: High-purity gases, silicon wafers, and copper for cooling/power.

tickers.desc: Global leader in industrial gases; provides ultra-high purity nitrogen and argon essential for chip fabrication.
Specializes in electronic gases and hydrogen; a key partner for on-site gas supply at mega-foundries.
Provides high-performance chemicals and filtration systems that ensure zero-defect chip manufacturing.
Major copper producer; supplies the raw material for data center power cabling and liquid cooling cold plates.
The world's largest producer of silicon wafers; the foundational substrate for all AI semiconductors.
The #2 global copper miner; copper is the critical material for data center power cabling and liquid-cooling cold plates.
Largest US steel producer; structural steel underpins every hyperscale data center build-out.
Engineers and manufactures cryogenic and industrial gas storage and delivery equipment used at chip fabs.
Provides gas control, power delivery, and plasma systems — the plumbing inside every semiconductor fab.
Only US-based rare-earth magnet producer; permanent magnets are critical for data center cooling fans and motors.
#1 US crushed-stone and aggregate producer; every hyperscale campus requires millions of tons of concrete, and Vulcan supplies the aggregate.
#2 US aggregates and cement supplier; directly exposed to the industrial construction boom driven by data center site development.
Major copper and zinc miner; copper demand is spiking from DC power infrastructure and liquid-cooling plumbing.
Produces specialty polymers and adhesive films used in advanced chip packaging encapsulants.
Largest global lithium producer; lithium-ion UPS systems are the critical backup power layer inside every tier-3 and tier-4 data center.

sectors.name: Specialty Energy & Equipment
sectors.description: Power and cooling infrastructure; the 2026 growth bottleneck.

tickers.desc: Provides grid-scale gas turbines and grid stabilization; the primary power provider for new data center hubs.
Global leader in thermal management; provides the liquid cooling systems required for high-density AI clusters.
Provides on-site fuel cell power; allows data centers to bypass long grid interconnection queues.
Largest uranium miner; the 'fuel' play for the resurgence of nuclear-powered data centers.
Designs high-density power conversion chips; enables the precise voltage regulation required by dense GPU clusters.
Designs and manufactures thermal management systems for data centers; growing adoption in liquid-cooled AI facilities.
Provides electrical enclosures and busway infrastructure; its products are specified into every hyperscale build.
Provides electrical switchgear and transformers; the literal hardware that connects a data center to the power grid.
Largest US nuclear operator; has signed direct power agreements with Microsoft and Amazon for dedicated data center capacity.
Owns nuclear and natural gas generation; rapidly expanding long-term capacity contracts with hyperscalers.
Global leader in commercial HVAC and cooling; data centers are its fastest-growing end market.
Industrial automation and process controls; acquired National Instruments — a key provider of power-management test systems.
Builds high-voltage electrical grid connections and substations that hyperscalers need to power new campuses.
World's largest renewable energy company; the primary developer of utility-scale solar and wind power purchase agreements for hyperscalers.
Largest US utility with operating nuclear plants; runs the most AI-contracted utility grid in the US across the Southeast.
The utility serving Virginia's Data Center Alley — the highest concentration of hyperscale capacity in the world.
Industrial compressed-air systems and precision cooling; its chiller and compression technology underpins mechanical cooling for large data centers.
Provides building management systems and HVAC for hyperscale campuses; its OpenBlue AI platform optimizes data center energy use in real time.

sectors.name: Fabless Chip Designers
sectors.description: AI architects designing accelerators and high-speed fabrics.

tickers.desc: The undisputed king of AI hardware; designs the H100 and B200 GPUs that power the generative AI revolution.
The primary challenger to NVIDIA; providing high-performance CPUs and GPUs for the open AI ecosystem.
Dominates AI networking and custom ASICs; the primary provider of the fabric that connects GPU clusters.
Designs mobile and data center AI silicon; its custom AI chips and NPUs target the edge computing market.
Provides custom AI silicon (XPUs) and optical interconnects; a key enabler of hyperscale AI networking.
Designs edge AI and automotive silicon; its secure processors are used in smart-building systems that manage data center access and HVAC.
Designs high-efficiency power-management ICs that regulate voltage inside every AI server rack — a direct GPU enabler.
Provides microcontrollers and FPGAs used in data center BMC, power sequencing, and fan control.
Fabless power semiconductor leader; its silicon-carbide and IGBT devices manage power delivery efficiency in data center PDUs.
Pure-play AI connectivity — makes the PCIe and CXL retimer chips that link GPU clusters to memory and storage at hyperscale.
Designs and manufactures optical transceivers and photonic ICs that carry data between GPU nodes in 400G and 800G AI networking fabrics.
Pure-play AI networking IC company; its SerDes and optical DSP chips are the signal-integrity layer inside 800G and 1.6T hyperscale switches.
Low-power FPGA designer; its chips power server baseboard management controllers and real-time inference at the edge.
Precision MEMS timing semiconductor company; every AI server requires ultra-stable clock references — SiTime is replacing quartz across hyperscale platforms.
Designs high-efficiency power conversion ICs for power supplies; widely used in the AC-DC and DC-DC conversion stages inside AI server PSUs.
Fabless designer of an optical interposer platform that co-packages lasers and electronics on a single chip; enables the next generation of energy-efficient 800G/1.6T transceivers.

sectors.name: Electronic Design Automation
sectors.description: Software and IP used to architect complex 2nm circuitry.

tickers.desc: The dominant software provider for chip design; essential for architecting complex 2nm AI processors.
Provides critical IP and design software; a primary gatekeeper for NVIDIA and AMD chip development.
Specializes in simulation software; used to model heat and structural integrity in high-density server racks.
The dominant chip architecture licensor; virtually every AI accelerator and mobile processor uses ARM IP.
Makes the electronic design simulation and signal-integrity test tools used to validate AI chip interconnects before tape-out.
Licenses memory interface IP (LPDDR, HBM) and security IP embedded in virtually every AI accelerator.
Provides analog and RF semiconductor components and IP for data center optical interconnects.
Metrology and inspection systems for advanced packaging — ensures yield in the HBM and chiplet assemblies used in AI accelerators.
Makes probe cards used to test every advanced AI chip at wafer level; demand scales directly with NVDA and AMD tape-out volumes.
Product lifecycle management and industrial simulation; tools used to design and validate the physical infrastructure of AI server hardware.
Process optimization software for industrial plants; used by chemical and gas suppliers feeding semiconductor fabs to maximize yield.
Fabless IoT and wireless connectivity IC designer; its chips enable the sensor networks monitoring temperature, power, and airflow in smart data centers.
AI vision SoC designer; its CV-series chips power the security cameras and physical intrusion detection systems across hyperscale campuses.
MOCVD and ion-beam deposition equipment used to grow compound semiconductor layers required for GaN and SiC power devices.
Develops electro-optic polymer platform technology for ultra-fast optical modulators; its IP enables co-packaged optics at densities and power levels silicon photonics cannot match.

sectors.name: Wafer Fab / Packaging Equipment
sectors.description: Precision machinery required for lithography and advanced packaging.

tickers.desc: Sole provider of EUV lithography machines; the only company capable of printing the world's most advanced AI chips.
Leader in etch and deposition equipment; critical for the 3D-stacking of HBM (High Bandwidth Memory).
The most diversified equipment maker; provides the machinery for nearly every step of the chip-making process.
Dominates the process control market; ensures high yields by detecting microscopic defects during fabrication.
Provides automated test equipment for semiconductors; the quality gatekeeper before AI chips are packaged and shipped.
Largest US-based OSAT; packages and tests a majority of AI chips after they leave TSMC — demand directly coupled to NVDA shipment volumes.
Advanced-packaging inspection and metrology; critical for HBM stacking yield used in H100 and B200 GPUs.
Ion implantation equipment — an essential fab process step for every logic and power device node.
Probe cards for wafer-level electrical testing; uniquely exposed to the high AI chip test volume ramp.
Semiconductor test handlers and thermal management systems; scales with AI chip unit volume.
High-power fiber lasers used for laser annealing, dicing, and cutting of advanced semiconductor wafers and substrates.
Automated wafer storage, transport, and handling systems; manages the physical flow of wafers through complex fab lines.
Manufactures precision gas delivery assemblies and frame modules built into AMAT, LRCX, and other equipment — a critical OEM supplier.
Dominant wire bonding and advanced packaging equipment supplier; its tools connect die to substrate in packages inside every AI accelerator.
Precision power conversion and control systems built into semiconductor etch and deposition equipment — enabling uniformity required for 3nm nodes.

sectors.name: Chip Fabs & Contract Manufacturers
sectors.description: Global foundries where advanced silicon nodes are printed.

tickers.desc: The world's foundry; manufactures over 90% of the world's most advanced AI accelerators for NVIDIA and Apple.
An integrated manufacturer pivoting to a foundry model; a key beneficiary of US-based 'CHIPS Act' funding.
The world's second-largest foundry and dominant DRAM/NAND maker; an integrated silicon giant essential to AI infrastructure.
Specializes in high-reliability chips for power management and automotive, essential for data center peripherals.
World's second-largest pure-play foundry; manufactures mature-node chips (power management, analog) essential to every AI server.
Largest global OSAT; performs advanced packaging for AI chips — indispensable assembly partner to TSMC.
Major OSAT with advanced packaging capacity; key back-end partner for AMD, NVIDIA, and Apple.
Specialty analog foundry producing power, RF, and CMOS image sensor chips inside every AI server rack.
The leading SiC substrate and device fab; silicon-carbide power devices are becoming the standard for data center power conversion.
Designs and manufactures gas and chemical delivery subsystems installed inside semiconductor fab tools — a critical enabler of advanced-node process chemistry.
Manufactures photomasks — the glass reticles used by every fab to pattern circuits; demand scales directly with new chip tape-out activity.
OSAT specializing in DRAM and display driver assembly and testing; tightly coupled to HBM and memory production cycles.
Precision RF and DC power conversion built into fab chambers; a de facto standard component inside every AMAT, LRCX, and TEL tool.
Supplies precision gas delivery modules and weldment assemblies to fabs and equipment makers; revenue moves in lockstep with wafer starts.

sectors.name: Integrated Device Manufacturers
sectors.description: Vertical giants that both design and manufacture their own silicon.

tickers.desc: An integrated manufacturer pivoting to a foundry model; a key beneficiary of US-based 'CHIPS Act' funding.
A leader in High Bandwidth Memory (HBM); provides the high-speed storage required to feed data to AI processors.
The dominant analog and embedded processor maker; designs the power management chips essential in every server and AI accelerator.
Provides mixed-signal and data conversion ICs; its precision analog chips manage power delivery in high-density AI server racks.
Designs and manufactures edge AI, automotive, and secure element chips; provides mixed-signal silicon for AI infrastructure endpoints.
Integrated power-management ICs — the voltage-regulation layer of every GPU server; fastest-growing power IC company.
Vertically integrated power semiconductor IDM; SiC and IGBT devices drive AI data center power efficiency.
European IDM with power, motion, and microcontroller lines; widely designed into server power management and cooling control.
IDM for RF and connectivity chips; its Wi-Fi and Bluetooth silicon is inside the management cards of virtually every AI server.
IDM for advanced RF semiconductors; its GaN-on-SiC power amplifiers are used in 5G small cells and O-RAN deployments adjacent to data centers.
Low-power FPGA IDM; its CrossLink and Mach series chips are the de facto solution for AI server BMC, re-timer, and real-time management applications.
Mixed-signal and power conversion IDM; designs high-precision ADC/DAC chips used in power delivery monitoring systems inside AI servers.
Magnetic sensor and power IC IDM; its current-sensing ICs are designed into every AI server PSU for real-time power monitoring.
Designs analog and mixed-signal ICs; its ClearEdge CDR chips are the timing and clock recovery components inside optical transceivers for AI networking.

sectors.name: Enterprise / Consumer Software
sectors.description: The monetization layer providing AI services and cloud compute.

tickers.desc: The leading enterprise AI platform via Azure and Copilot; the largest single buyer of NVIDIA GPUs globally.
Designs its own TPU AI accelerators and operates Google Cloud; a vertically integrated AI infrastructure leader.
Operates AWS, the world's largest cloud; also designs its own Trainium and Inferentia AI chips for cost advantage.
Provides the AIP platform that enterprises use to operationalize large language models on proprietary data.
Provides enterprise database and cloud infrastructure; deploying massive GPU clusters for AI workloads.
Provides creative AI software (Firefly); transitioning its Creative Cloud to AI-native workflows.
The cloud data platform acting as the AI data layer; integrates directly with LLMs for enterprise AI applications.
The largest AI infrastructure investor outside cloud titans; designs its own MTIA inference chips and trains Llama models at extreme scale.
Enterprise AI platform (Einstein, Agentforce); drives GPU cloud demand and is the template for AI-native enterprise SaaS.
Leading enterprise AI automation platform; its Now Assist agents run on GPU infrastructure with rapidly expanding compute consumption.
AI/ML observability platform; directly measures and monitors GPU utilization and LLM cost — the ops layer for AI infrastructure.
Document database powering vector search and AI application data layers; Atlas Vector Search is foundational AI data infrastructure.
World's largest enterprise software company; its SAP Business AI and Joule assistant are being deployed across tens of thousands of corporate AI instances globally.
AI-first financial software platform (TurboTax, QuickBooks); its GenAI financial assistant is one of the most widely used consumer AI products.
AI-powered cybersecurity platform protecting data center perimeters; Precision AI detects threats across network, endpoint, and cloud layers.
Enterprise AI for HR and finance; its Illuminate AI assistant runs on hyperscaler GPU infrastructure and is a proxy for enterprise AI adoption velocity.
Network-as-a-service and edge AI inference platform; Workers AI lets enterprises run LLM inference on its global edge network.

sectors.name: Tech Equipment / Devices
sectors.description: Final rack integration, networking fabric, and enterprise hardware.

tickers.desc: Designs its own silicon (M-series) for AI inference at the edge; a key driver of on-device AI adoption at scale.
Assembles AI servers (PowerEdge) with NVIDIA GPUs; the primary VAR selling full AI rack solutions to enterprises.
The fastest-growing AI server assembler; specializes in liquid-cooled, GPU-dense rack solutions for hyperscale data centers.
Provides the networking fabric (switches, routers) that interconnects GPU clusters for high-speed AI workloads.
Provides complete AI compute systems (GreenLake) with integrated liquid cooling; growing in HPC and AI supercomputing.
A contract electronics manufacturer assembling AI servers for Microsoft, Google, and Amazon at scale.
Dominant AI data center networking switch vendor; Meta, Microsoft, and Google have named Arista primary supplier for 800G AI fabric.
Provides enterprise AI inference hardware (Telum II), quantum systems, and hybrid cloud AI infrastructure for regulated industries.
All-flash and hybrid storage optimized for AI training datasets; StorageGRID is the object store layer under hyperscale AI pipelines.
All-flash storage arrays purpose-built for AI/ML workloads; key vendor displacing HDD-based storage at AI labs and hyperscalers.
Contract electronics manufacturer assembling AI servers and rack systems for hyperscalers; direct complement to CLS.
Industrial automation and building management; its data center solutions division provides environmental monitoring and BMS for the world's largest campuses.
AI-optimized application delivery and load balancing; routes inference traffic across GPU clusters and provides security for public AI APIs.
Coherent optical networking equipment connecting hyperscale data centers; 800G WaveLogic modem is the backbone of inter-DC AI traffic.
Designs and manufactures optical transceivers and laser chips for AI data center interconnects; demand tied to the 400G/800G upgrade cycle.
Makes high-speed optical transceiver modules purpose-built for hyperscale data center interconnects; a pure-play beneficiary of the 400G/800G upgrade cycle.
Network and fiber test and measurement equipment; used to qualify optical links inside hyperscale AI clusters and certify new fiber builds.
